query: What is J.P. Morgan Private Bank's mid-year 2026 outlook on global fragmentation, inflation, and AI, and what are the investment implications?

source_pack.name: jpm_private_bank_midyear_outlook_2026
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: JPMorgan_wealth_management_strategy
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

source_pack.name: US_private_bank_research
source_pack.notes: Source candidate inferred from report content.

key_facts.claim: Global fragmentation and the end of the peace dividend have raised the cost of stability, reshaping opportunities in select emerging markets, gold, defense, and companies strengthening domestic resilience.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.fact: Global fragmentation and the end of the peace dividend have raised the cost of stability, reshaping opportunities in select emerging markets, gold, defense, and companies strengthening domestic resilience.

key_facts.claim: Energy shocks are adding to stickier inflation; real assets and select hedge fund strategies should be considered for diversification beyond traditional stocks and bonds.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Part 2: Inflation – sections on energy shock and portfolio implications
key_facts.fact: Energy shocks are adding to stickier inflation; real assets and select hedge fund strategies should be considered for diversification beyond traditional stocks and bonds.

key_facts.claim: The AI supercycle continues with record adoption driving productivity gains; investors should consider data center beneficiaries, explore private markets, and avoid vulnerable legacy software.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.fact: The AI supercycle continues with record adoption driving productivity gains; investors should consider data center beneficiaries, explore private markets, and avoid vulnerable legacy software.

key_facts.claim: A blockade of Taiwan could result in a -5% shock to global GDP growth; U.S. GDP could fall 6.7%, comparable to the global financial crisis.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Part 1: Fragmentation – downside scenario 'Energy and semiconductor bottlenecks close'
key_facts.fact: A blockade of Taiwan could result in a -5% shock to global GDP growth; U.S. GDP could fall 6.7%, comparable to the global financial crisis.

key_facts.claim: Traditional 60% stock/40% bond allocation is under pressure due to persistent inflation and the narrowing gap between cash yields and price inflation.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Key takeaways and Introduction; reference to 'pressure' on traditional portfolios
key_facts.fact: Traditional 60% stock/40% bond allocation is under pressure due to persistent inflation and the narrowing gap between cash yields and price inflation.

key_facts.claim: Labor market slack could counter rising prices, and tariffs/energy shocks may prove temporary, with housing disinflation being durable.
key_facts.why_it_matters: Important benchmark fact from the source report.
key_facts.verification_hint: Part 2: Inflation – 'What could go right?' section
key_facts.fact: Labor market slack could counter rising prices, and tariffs/energy shocks may prove temporary, with housing disinflation being durable.
